ocr.text: In the context of the evolving landscape of the oil and gas sector, particularly within the Asia-Pacific region, the throughput of
Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) export terminals is critically linked to economic and operational variables. The accompanying
data table provides a comprehensive analysis of LNG terminal throughput in tons alongside projected decommissioning
liabilities estimated in million SGD for the years 2023 and 2024.
LNGExportVolume  TotalThroughput
TerminalCapacity  UtilizationRate
QuarterlyPerformance  SGD
Tons  MillionSGD  Percentage(%)
Notably, these estimates reflect a substantial increase in anticipated decommissioning costs, rising from SGD1.2B in 2023 to
SGD1.3B in 2024, indicative of the growing regulatory and environmental considerations surrounding aging infrastructure.

Additionally, the sensitivity analysis demonstrates how fluctuating oil prices impact terminal operations, with base price
scenarios highlighting a critical threshold at SGD100M. This analysis underscores the importance of strategic planning in
financial forecasting, particularly as operators navigate the complexities of market volatility and the associated financial
implications for decommissioning projects.

rapidly changing energy landscape.
